title: Find angles in isosceles triangles
description: Find missing angles in isosceles triangles given just one angle.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $14)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $50^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

>$x^\circ + 50^\circ + 50^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + 50 + 50 &= 180 \\\\
x + 100 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
x + 100 - \blueD{100} &= 180 - \blueD{100} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 100 from each side}}}\\\\
x &= 80 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 80^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of eighteen, an angle of fifty degrees and its opposite side has a length of fourteen, and its third side has a length of fourteen.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $12)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $62^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + 62^\circ + 62^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + 62 + 62 &= 180 \\\\
x + 124 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
x + 124 - \blueD{124} &= 180 - \blueD{124} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 124 from each side}}}\\\\
x &= 56 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 56^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of ten point four, an angle of sixty-two degrees and its opposite side has a length of twelve, and its third side has a length of twelve.

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $7)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $66^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$\begin{align} x + 66 + 66 &= 180 \\\\

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $6.2)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $61^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + 61^\circ + 61^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + 61 + 61 &= 180 \\\\
x + 122 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
x + 122 - \blueD{122} &= 180 - \blueD{122} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 122 from each side}}}\\\\
x &= 58 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 58^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of six, an angle of sixty-one degrees and its opposite side has a length of six point two, and its third side has a length of six point two.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $3.5)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $55^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + 55^\circ + 55^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + 55 + 55 &= 180 \\\\
x + 110 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
x + 110 - \blueD{110} &= 180 - \blueD{110} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 110 from each side}}}\\\\
x &= 70 \end{align}$

###The answer

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of four, an angle of fifty-five degrees and its opposite side has a length of three point five, and its third side has a length of three point five.

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $4)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $56^\circ$:

###Step 2: Writing an equation

We can write this as an equation:

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $3.3)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $39^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + 39^\circ + 39^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + 39 + 39 &= 180 \\\\
x + 78 &= 180 &\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}\\\\
x + 78 - \blueD{78} &= 180 - \blueD{78} &\gray{\text{Subtract 78 from each side}}\\\\
x &= 102 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 102^\circ$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $7)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $x^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + x^\circ + 80^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + x + 80 &= 180 \\\\
2x + 80 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
2x + 80 - \blueD{80} &= 180 - \blueD{80} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 80 from each side}}}\\\\
2x &= 100 \\\\
\dfrac{2x}{\blueD2} &= \dfrac{100}{\blueD2} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Divide each side by 2}}}\\\\ \\\\
x &= 50 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 50^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of seven, an angle of eighty degrees and its opposite side has a length of nine, and its third side has a length of seven.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $6)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $x^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + x^\circ + 56^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + x + 56 &= 180 \\\\
2x + 56 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
2x + 56 - \blueD{56} &= 180 - \blueD{56} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 56 from each side}}}\\\\
2x &= 124 \\\\
\dfrac{2x}{\blueD2} &= \dfrac{124}{\blueD2} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Divide each side by 2}}}\\\\ \\\\
x &= 62 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 62^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of six, an angle of fifty-six degrees and its opposite side has a length of five point seven, and its third side has a length of six.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $5)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $x^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + x^\circ + 70^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + x + 70 &= 180 \\\\
2x + 70 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
2x + 70 - \blueD{70} &= 180 - \blueD{70} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 70 from each side}}}\\\\
2x &= 110 \\\\
\dfrac{2x}{\blueD2} &= \dfrac{110}{\blueD2} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Divide each side by 2}}}\\\\ \\\\
x &= 55 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 55^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of five, an angle of seventy degrees and its opposite side has a length of five point seven, and its third side has a length of five.

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $6)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $x^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + x^\circ + 58^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + x + 58 &= 180 \\\\
2x + 58 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
2x + 58 - \blueD{58} &= 180 - \blueD{58} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 58 from each side}}}\\\\

###The answer

$x = 61^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of six, an angle of fifty-eight degrees and its opposite side has a length of five point eight, and its third side has a length of six.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $5)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $x^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + x^\circ + 88^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + x + 88 &= 180 \\\\
2x + 88 &= 180 ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}}\\\\
2x + 88 - \blueD{88} &= 180 - \blueD{88} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Subtract 88 from each side}}}\\\\
2x &= 92 \\\\
\dfrac{2x}{\blueD2} &= \dfrac{92}{\blueD2} ~~~~~~~~\small{\gray{\text{Divide each side by 2}}}\\\\ \\\\
x &= 46 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 46^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of five, an angle of eighty-eight degrees and its opposite side has a length of six point nine, and its third side has a length of five.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $7.2)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $x^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + x^\circ + 32^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + x + 32 &= 180 \\\\
2x + 32 &= 180 &\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}\\\\
2x + 32 - \blueD{32} &= 180 - \blueD{32} &\gray{\text{Subtract 32 from each side}}\\\\
2x &= 148 \\\\
\dfrac{2x}{\blueD2} &= \dfrac{148}{\blueD2} &\gray{\text{Divide each side by 2}}\\\\
x &= 74 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 74^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of seven point two, an angle of thirty-two degrees and its opposite side has a length of four, and its third side has a length of seven point two.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the value of $x$ in the triangle shown below.**

$x = $ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
###Step 1: Recognizing that we have an isosceles triangle

This is an isosceles triangle because it has two congruent sides $($the two sides with a length of $10)$. The angles across from the congruent sides are also congruent, so the angle without a label measures $x^\circ$:



[[☃ image 1]]

###Step 2: Writing an equation

The three angles in a triangle add up to $180 ^\circ$.
We can write this as an equation:

$x^\circ + x^\circ + 116^\circ = 180^\circ$

###Step 3: Solving for $x$

$\begin{align} x + x + 116 &= 180 \\\\
2x + 116 &= 180 &\gray{\text{Simplify the left-hand side}}\\\\
2x + 116 - \blueD{116} &= 180 - \blueD{116} &\gray{\text{Subtract 116 from each side}}\\\\
2x &= 64 \\\\
\dfrac{2x}{\blueD2} &= \dfrac{64}{\blueD2} &\gray{\text{Divide each side by 2}}\\\\
x &= 32 \end{align}$

###The answer

$x = 32^\circ$

options.alt: A triangle with angle x degrees and its opposite side has a length of ten, an angle of one hundred sixteen degrees and its opposite side has a length of seventeen, and its third side has a length of ten.
